edges.description: The fistulous low-resistance connection diverts blood away from the myocardial capillary network and can impose volume load on the drainage site.
edges.source: Abnormal coronary artery-to-chamber or vessel shunt

nodes.description: Developmental or acquired disruption of coronary vascular anatomy creates a persistent communication from a coronary artery to a cardiac chamber, great vessel, or other structure, bypassing the myocardial capillary bed.
nodes.id: Abnormal coronary artery-to-chamber or vessel shunt

evidence.reference_title: Coronary artery fistulas: clinical and therapeutic considerations.
evidence.snippet: These fistulas are congenital or acquired coronary artery abnormalities in which blood is shunted into a cardiac chamber, great vessel, or other structure, bypassing the myocardial capillary network.

nodes.description: Medium or large fistulas can enlarge and produce coronary steal, distal myocardial ischemia, chamber enlargement, arrhythmia, myocardial infarction, or congestive heart failure.

evidence.reference_title: Coronary Artery Fistulas: Indications, Techniques, Outcomes, and Complications of Transcatheter Fistula Closure.
evidence.snippet: Small CAFs are generally asymptomatic and can close over time, while some untreated medium or large CAFs can enlarge, leading to clinical sequelae such as cardiac chamber enlargement or myocardial ischemia.
